AeAssessment.description:
As part of AutoEnrolment we assess your Employees to see if they need to be auto-enroled in a Pension.
This model shows the result of an assessment.

age.description: [readonly] The age of the Employee at the time of the assessment

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

ordinal.description: [readonly] The PaySchedule ordinal for the Employee at the time of the assessment

payRunVersion.description: [readonly] The supplementary pay run version of this AeAssessment.

reason.description: [readonly] The reason for determining the Status given.

actionCompleted.description: [readonly] Indicates whether or not the required action was successfully completed

actionCompletedMessage.description: [readonly] Gives further information about the action taken or the reason if wasn't successfully completed

letterNotYetSent.description: [readonly] Indicates whether or not any required letter has been sent

pensionSchemeId.description: [readonly] The PensionSchemeId that a completed action relates to

workerGroupId.description: [readonly] The WorkerGroupId that a completed action relates to

color.description: A color to used to represent this Analysis Code, in hex format. ie 'ff0000'

title.description: The name of this Analysis Code

AttachmentOrder.description: Used to represent an Attachment of Earnings Order (AEO)

allowProtectedEarnings.description: [ReadOnly] Whether or not shortfalls should be carried forward

amountPreviouslyPaid.description: Any amount that has previously been paid towards this Order

applyFrom.description: The date from which to apply this Order

applyUntil.description: An optional date on which to stop applying this order

autoDeduction.description: [readonly] Whether or not the amount to be deducted is automatically calculated

carryForwardShortfall.description: [ReadOnly] Whether or not shortfalls should be carried forward

chargeAdminFee.description: Whether or not a £1 admin fee should be deducted for this order

deduction.description: The percentage or amount (depending on DeductionIsPercentage) to deduct

deductionIsPercentage.description: Whether or not the amount given for Deduction is a percentage rather than a fixed amount

documentCount.description: [readonly] The number of attachments associated with this model

documents.description: [readonly] The attachments associated with this model

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

issueDate.description: The date of issue for this Order

notes.description: A free-form text field to record any comments

payee.description: The Id of the Payee, if any, that deductions are to be paid to.

payeeName.description: The name of the Payee, if any, that deductions are to be paid to.

payments.description: [readonly] Payments made towards this order. Only populated when viewed as a report.

protectedEarnings.description: The percentage or amount or percentage (depending on ProtectedEarningsIsPercentage) to protect

protectedEarningsIsPercentage.description: Whether or not the amount for ProtectedEarnings is a percentage rather than a fixed amount.

reference.description: The reference which appeared on the court paperwork

shortfall.description: Any shortfall that is being carried forward

stopWhenTotalPaid.description: Whether or not this order should be stopped once a total amount has been paid

stopped.description:
[readonly] Whether or not this Order has been stopped.
This is set automatically when either it's paid in full or the ApplyUntil date has been reached.

totalAmountToPay.description: The Total amount that needs to be paid for this Order

AttachmentOrderPayment.description: Used to represent a payment towards an Attachment of Earnings Order (AEO)

attachablePay.description: [readonly] The total Attachable pay for the Employee on this PayRun

date.description: [readonly] The date this deduction was made

description.description: [readonly] Description of deduction, usually the reference from the AttachmentOrder

desiredDeduction.description:
[readonly] The amount that should be deducted, if arrestable pay is available.
Not including any shortfall

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isLatestVersion.description: [readonly] Indicates whether this is the latest version of the AttachmentOrderPayment.

otherAttachmentOrderDeductions.description: [readonly] Deductions made on this PayRun for this employee due to  other higher priority AttachmentOrders

payRunVersion.description: [readonly] The supplementary pay run version of this AttachmentOrderPayment.

protectedPay.description: [readonly] The amount of the Attachable Pay that must be protected

resultingShortfall.description: [readonly] The resulting shortfall (including any shortfall from previous periods

shortfall.description: [readonly] Any shortfall (ie arrears) that existed before this PayRun.

totalPaid.description: [readonly] Total amount paid to date, including this deduction.

daysToDeferAssessment.description:
The number of days, if any, to defer assessment of this employee.
You're allowed to defer assessment of new employees by up to 3 months.

deferByMonthsNotDays.description: If set to true then the value in DaysToDeferAssessment will be treated as a number of months, not a number of days

postponementDate.description:
[readonly] If a value is present, then employee wont be enrolled on an AE Pension until after this date.
This is automatically set to a date if the employee is deemed to be an EligibleJobHolder, but the employer has set a Postponement period and this value is currently null
This is automatically set to null if it already has a value and the employee is deemed NOT to be an EligibleJobHolder - ie, they previously qualified but now do not.

AutomationSettings.description: Configures various automation settings for an Employer

autoPilotOffset.description:
How many days before the payment date a payrun should be finalised.
Set it to 0 if you want the payrun to be automatically finalised on the payment date itself

autoSubmitEps.description: If set to true, we'll automatically send your EPS to HMRC whenever you finalise a PayRun.

autoSubmitFps.description:
If set to true, we'll automatically send your FPS to HMRC whenever you finalise a PayRun.
This property will always have the same value as the property with the same name on the RtiSubmissionSettings model.

autoSubmitJournal.description:
If set to true, we'll automatically submit the payroll journal whenever you finalise a PayRun.
The employer must be connected to an ExternalDataProvider supporting Type of 'Accounting'.

autoSubmitP45s.description:
If set to true, we'll automatically submit P45s whenever you finalise a PayRun.
The employer must be connected to an ExternalDataProvider supporting Type of 'EmployeePortal'.

autoSubmitPayments.description:
If set to true, we'll automatically submit payments whenever you finalise a PayRun.
The employer must be connected to an ExternalDataProvider supporting Type of 'Payments'.

autoSubmitPayslips.description:
If set to true, we'll automatically submit payslips whenever you finalise a PayRun.
The employer must be connected to an ExternalDataProvider supporting Type of 'EmployeePortal'.

autoSubmitPensionLetters.description:
If set to true, we'll automatically submit Pension Letters whenever you finalise a PayRun.
The employer must be connected to an ExternalDataProvider supporting Type of 'EmployeePortal'.

codingNoticesAutomaticallyApplied.description: [readonly] An indicator of whether or not this employer is automatically applying DpsNotices

employeesWithEmailAddressButNotAutoSendi.description: [readonly] A count of how many employees or subcontractors for this employer do have email addresses but don't have the option enabled to auto-email payslips

employeesWithoutEmailAddress.description: [readonly] A count of how many employees or subcontractors for this employer do not have email addresses

enableAutoOpen.description: IF enabled then whenever you close a payrun, the next one will be started. Automatically set to true if EnableAutoPilot is true

enableAutoPilot.description: IF enabled then payruns will be automatically finalised on the payment date and the next payrun will be started

payrunEmails.description: Automated emails that will be sent when a PayRun is finalised

subcontractorsNotAutoSendingStatement.description: [readonly] A count of how many subscontractors for this employer do don't have the option enabled to auto-email statement

firstName.description: The First Name of the employee

lastName.description: The Last Name of the employee

period.description: The Period of the average holiday pay rate

Benefit.description: Used to represent Benefits and Expenses

endDate.description: The date the benefits ends if different to the end date of the TaxYear

openingBalance.description: The amount of benefit paid YTD when setting up a benefit

overrideOpeningBalance.description: To be used to change the opening balance if the benefit has been payrolled

startDate.description: The date the benefits starts if different to the start date of the TaxYear

tradingOrganisation.description: Only relevant to Benefits with Type Entertainment

chargeableValue.description: [readonly] The chargeable value of the car

fullYearCharge.description: [readonly] The charge for the car for a full year, not taking in to account available dates or EmployeePrivateContributions

rate.description: [readonly] The applicable rate based on CO2Emissions and Engine Size

isLatestVersion.description: [readonly] Indicates whether this is the latest version of the BenefitPayrolled.

isBacsClient.description: Indicates that the bureau is responsible for BACS payments of net wages to employees

isBacsClientForHmrc.description: Indicates that the bureau is responsible for BACS payments of HMRC liabilites

processorUserId.description: The Id of the user, if any, that is the allocated Processor

reportPackId.description: The Id of the ReportPack, if any, to present to the Payroll Client

CarCharge.description:
Part of the TaxYearConfig that our engine uses to calculate charges for a Company Car.
It is used internally when our engine performs calculations.
You do not need to do anything with this model, it's provided purely for informational purposes.

Part of the TaxYearConfig that our engine uses to calculate charges for a Company Car.
It is used internally when our engine performs calculations.

This Id is used to delete the previous audit entry once the change summary entry is created
for the modifications between the current and the previous audit entry.

uniqueId.description: Unique id of the owner this request belongs to

Analysis Category identifier

daysToDeferAssessment.description:
The number of days, if any, to defer assessment of this employee.
You're allowed to defer assessment of new employees by up to 3 months.

deferByMonthsNotDays.description: If set to true then the value in DaysToDeferAssessment will be treated as a number of months, not a number of days

daysToDeferAssessment.description:

postponementDate.description:
[readonly] If a value is present, then employee wont be enrolled on an AE Pension until after this date.
This is automatically set to a date if the employee is deemed to be an EligibleJobHolder, but the employer has set a Postponement period and this value is currently null
This is automatically set to null if it already has a value and the employee is deemed NOT to be an EligibleJobHolder - ie, they previously qualified but now do not.

daysToDeferAssessment.description:
The number of days, if any, to defer assessment of new employees.
You're allowed to defer assessment of new employees by up to 3 months.
This is the default value used when you create a new employee. It can be changed on a per-employee basis.

deferByMonthsNotDays.description: If set to true then the value in DaysToDeferAssessment will be treated as a number of months, not a number of days

deferEnrolmentBy.description:
The number of days )or weeks, or months), if any, to defer enrolment of employees that are Eligible Jobholders.
For example, if this is set to 30 days then if an employee meets the criteria for enrolment then they'll only be enrolled if they still meet the criteria 30 days later

exemptFromAeRegulations.description: Indicates if an employer is exempt from normal AE regulations

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

includeNonPensionedEmployeesInSubmission.description: Whether or not to include details of non-pensioned employees in your submissions to this provider

deferByMonthsNotDays.description: If set to true then the value in DaysToDeferAssessment will be treated as a number of months, not a number of days

deferEnrolmentBy.description:
The number of days )or weeks, or months), if any, to defer enrolment of employees that are Eligible Jobholders.
For example, if this is set to 30 days then if an employee meets the criteria for enrolment then they'll only be enrolled if they still meet the criteria 30 days later

exemptFromAeRegulations.description: Indicates if an employer is exempt from normal AE regulations

includeNonPensionedEmployeesInSubmission.description: Whether or not to include details of non-pensioned employees in your submissions to this provider

adjustment.description:
Adjustment to number of hours/days/weeks holiday this employee can take per year.
Will reset to 0 when the Allowance resets.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

allowance.description:
The number of days holiday an employee can take per year if HolidayType is Days.
Otherwise this is readonly and gives you the number of days accrued since the last reset

allowanceResetDate.description: The date that the holiday allowance resets. Only the day/month part of the value is relevant.

maximumAccruePeriod.description: The maximum number of hours capable of being accrued in a single period

To recalculate set to null with annual amount recorded in Value

code.description: The Code of the PayCode this line is assigned to. The PayCode determines the treatment of this line when it comes to NI, Tax and Pensions as well as whether it's a deduction or addition.

ignoreInitialBackPay.description:
Skip the automatic backpay calculation on the first pay run, if the addition/deduction had been paid outside or Staffology.
If set to false, the automatic backpay calculation will be performed on the first pay run.

isAutomaticBackPay.description:
Automatically calculate backpay and add or modify the necessary pay lines for this addition/deduction when a pay run is created.
Applicable only to employee level addition/deduction pay lines.
If set to false then you must manually handle the calculation and adding of backpay lines.

isNetToGross.description: If the PayLine is a fixed ammount addition without multiplier then this property may be set to true so that the amount of the addition to be considered a take home pay target.

londonAllowanceId.description: Used to set London allowance id for payline

multiplier.description: If the related PayCode has a MultiplierType other than None then this field will be used as number of days or hours

netToGrossDiscrepancy.description: The discrepancy between the targeted and the calculated grossed up value durig a net to gross calculation.

rate.description: If the related  PayCode has a MultiplierType other than None then this field will be used as the rate per day or hour. Otherwise it isn't used

salaryFormula.description:
Formula used to derive annual salary
<remarks>
The salary formula consists of the following elements and Round functions:
- PointValue (The value of an individual Spinal Point)
- HoursWorked (The hours an employee is contracted to work for in their role)
- FTHours (The full time hours specified for an employee in their role)
- WeeksWorked (The contracted weeks an employee works in their role)
- FTWeeks (The full weeks specified for an employee in their role)
Round functions: 
- ROUND(Value,decimal places): Round the value to specific decimal places. The maximum value is between 0 and 15.
- ROUNDUP(Value)
- ROUNDDOWN(Value) 
- TRUNCATE(Value)
</remarks><example>
For example: "PointValue * ROUND(HoursWorked * WeeksWorked * (7/365),3) / FTHours" 
</example>

content.description: [readonly] This could contain a link to a PDF file, HTML content or other content, depending on the Type value.

model.description: [readonly] If the type is application.json then this will contain a JSON representation of the relevant model

type.description: [readonly] The content-type, this would usually be the same as the accept header you provided when you requested the report

defaultPensionSchemeUniqueId.description: Used when employee role has attached default pension scheme Id

defaultWorkerGroupUniqueId.description: Used when employee role has attached default worker group Id

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isPrimary.description: Set to True if this is Primary role of the Employee

occupationalMaternityPolicyUniqueId.description: Used for assigning occupational maternity policy

occupationalSicknessPolicyUniqueId.description: Used for assigning occupational sickness policy

workingPatternId.description:
Used when calculating payments for Leave.
If null then the default Working Pattern is used

isPrimary.description:
Set to true if this is the primary AnalysisCategoryCode for the Employee.
Only one AnalysisCategoryCode can be set as the primary.

weighting.description:
If there is more than one AnalysisCategoryCode Membership for the Employee then this determines the weighting to give to this membership.
ie, if he is in two AnalysisCategoryCode you might set the primary as 0.8 and the secondary as 0.2;

baseDailyRate.description:
This property is used to calculate values for PayCodes that are set as multiples of 
the employees base daily rate. Eg sick.
If this is set as zero then we'll attempt to calculate a value based on the other fields

baseHourlyRate.description:
This property is used to calculate values for PayCodes that are set as multiples of 
the employees base hourly rate. Eg Overtime.
If this is set as zero then we'll attempt to calculate a value based on the other fields

weight.description:
This property is used to calculate values for the relative weight of the usual pay for 
each role compared to the sum of all roles' usual pay
Usual pay for the purpose of the current value's calculation is based on the employee's permanent pay items
including employee basic pay and permanent addition and deduction pay lines subject to NI or Tax

baseDailyRate.description:
This property is used to calculate values for PayCodes that are set as multiples of 
the employees base daily rate. Eg sick.
If this is set as zero then we'll attempt to calculate a value based on the other fields

baseHourlyRate.description:
This property is used to calculate values for PayCodes that are set as multiples of 
the employees base hourly rate. Eg Overtime.
If this is set as zero then we'll attempt to calculate a value based on the other fields

basicPay.description:
The amount of basic pay the Employee is regularly paid each period
This amount is the combined total of all pay elements that contribute to basic pay

excludedRegularPayLines.description: These used to store the PayLines that have been excluded from RegularPaylines.

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

mapsMiles.description: The number of miles to pay for as Mileage Allowance Payments

payAmount.description: The amount the Employee is regularly paid each period

payAmountMultiplier.description:
This property is irrelevant if the basis is Monthly.
But if the basis is Daily or Hourly then this property sets how many days/hours the employee should be paid for in the period.

payCode.description: If you want the Employees pay to be allocated to a code other than BASIC, specify it here

regularPayLines.description:
These are used to make additions/deductions to the pay for this Employee.
You do not need to include Pension, Tax, NI,  Loan Repayments, etc as these will all be automatically created.

EmployerDefaults.description:
When a user creates a new Employer, certain settings can be copied from an existing employer.
This model determines which employer (if any) settings should be copied from and what should be copied.

csvMappings.description: If true then CSV Mappings will be copied from the specified Employer

departments.description: If true then Departments, Cost Centres and Analysis Categories will be copied from the specified Employer

hmrcNoticeSettings.description: If true then HMRC Notice Settings will be copied from the specified Employer

occupationalPolicies.description: If true then Occupational Absence Schemes will be copied from the specified Employer.

payCodes.description: If true then PayCodes and PayCodeSets will be copied from the specified Employer

payOptions.description: If true then Default Pay Options will be copied from the specified Employer.

payees.description: If true then Payees will be copied from the specified Employer

pensionSchemes.description: If true then PensionSchemes will be copied from the specified Employer

reportPacks.description: If true then ReportPacks will be copied from the specified Employer

rti.description: If true then RTI Settings will be copied from the specified Employer

users.description: If true then Users will be copied from the specified Employer

workingPatterns.description: If true then WorkingPatterns will be copied from the specified Employer

Part of the TaxYearConfig that our engine uses to enable calculation of Tax and NI.
It is used internally when our engine performs calculations.
You do not need to do anything with this model, it's provided purely for informational purposes.

overridePaymentDate.description:
If a value if provided here then it will be used in place of the actual PaymentDate on the FPS.
The value must fall in to the same Tax Period as the PaymentDate

versions.description:
If any of the payments on this FPS appear on another FPS then
then there will be an entry here for each of them.
If there is an entry where the FpsId matches this FPS then that means the FpsEmployee on this Fps
is itself a prior version (ie, it's been superseded)

If set to true then any unpaid amounts from previous periods will be brought forward
to work out the liability for the current period.
You'd set this to false if you don't want to track payments.

and is claiming EmploymentAllowance, then set this to true to include a line for
Employment Allowance on the journal for the monthly schedule.

quarterlyPaymentSchedule.description: Set to true if the employer pays HMRC on a quarterly schedule. A value of false implies a monthly schedule. Warning: Changing this value after starting PayRuns will delete any existing payments or adjustments you may have entered.

allowLinkedEps.description: If true this will allow you to submit a combined Employer Payment Summary

apprenticeshipLevy.description: Amount due with respect to the Apprenticeship Levy

apprenticeshipLevyAuto.description:
If set to true, we'll automatically calculate your liability
If set to false then you can enter your own value.
If LiableForApprenticeshipLevy is false then the ApprenticeshipLevy will 
always be overwritten with a zero value regardless of this property

apprenticeshipLevyEpsCreated.description:
[readonly] If there is an Apprenticeship LEvy amount present and the Period is complete then this field Indicates 
whether or not an EPS has been created up to the final tax month of this period to declare
the amount

cisDeductions.description: [readonly] Amount due to HMRC for CIS Deductions made in period

dueInPreviousPeriods.description: [readonly] Amount due to HMRC in previous periods for this Tax Year

eligibleForEmploymentAllowance.description: [readonly] Indicates whether the Employer settings say the Employer is eligible for Employment Allowance

employeeNic.description: [readonly] Amount due to HMRC for Employee NIC Contributions withheld in period

employerNic.description: [readonly] Amount due to HMRC for Employer NIC Contributions due on payments in period

employmentAllowanceClaim.description: Amount to claim with respect to the Employment Allowance

employmentAllowanceClaimAuto.description:
If set to true, we'll automatically claim the maximum allowed.
If set to false then you can enter your own value.
If EligibleForEmploymentAllowance is false then the EmploymentAllowanceClaim will 
always be overwritten with a zero value regardless of this property

employmentAllowanceClaimInPreviousPeriod.description: [readonly] The amount of Employment Allowance claimed in previous periods for this Tax Year

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

liabilityArisingInPeriod.description: [readonly] Total Liability (before any deductions) arising in this Period

liableForApprenticeshipLevy.description: [readonly] Indicates whether the Employer settings say the Employer is liable for the Apprenticeship Levy

netLiabilityForPeriod.description: [readonly] Net Liability (including any deductions/additions) arising in this Period

paidInPreviousPeriods.description: [readonly] Amount paid to HMRC in previous periods for this Tax Year

periodComplete.description: [readonly] Indicates whether or not all PayRuns for this period have been completed

postgradLoanDeductions.description: [readonly] Amount due to HMRC for Postgrad Loan Deductions made in period

realTimeClass1ANic.description: [readonly] Amount due to HMRC for Employer NIC Contributions due on payments in period

receivedFromHMRCToPayStatutoryPay.description: Any amount received from HMRC to pay Statutory Pay Period

receivedFromHMRCToRefundTax.description: Any amount received from HMRC to refund Tax in Period

recoverableAmountsEpsCreated.description:
[readonly] If there are recoverable amount present (ie, SMP) and the Period is complete then this field Indicates 
whether or not an EPS has been created up to the final tax month of this period to declare
the recoverable amounts

sap.description: [readonly] SAP recovered from payments in period

shpp.description: [readonly] ShPP recovered from payments in period

smp.description: [readonly] SMP recovered from payments in period

sncp.description: [readonly] SNCP recovered from payments in period

spbp.description: [readonly] SPBP recovered from payments in period

spp.description: [readonly] SPP recovered from payments in period

studentLoanDeductions.description: [readonly] Amount due to HMRC for Student Loan Deductions made in period

tax.description: [readonly] Amount due to HMRC for PAYE in period

title.description: [readonly] A descriptive name for the Period

totalPaid.description: [readonly] The total value of all payments in this period

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

message.description: An optional message to include in the email sent to the EmailAddress

Used to encapsulate a response for any of the reports.
See the Introduction Guide for Reports for more details

content.description: [readonly] This could contain a link to a PDF file, HTML content or other content, depending on the Type value.

model.description: [readonly] If the type is application.json then this will contain a JSON representation of the relevant model

type.description: [readonly] The content-type, this would usually be the same as the accept header you provided when you requested the report

content.description: Content of Journal Template

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

useColumnHeaders.description: Use Column Headers indicates whether to use column headers while parsing the content in CSV format

Leave.description: Used to represent Leave, including Holiday and Statutory leave (such as Maternity Leave)

assumedPensionablePays.description: if UseAssumedPensionablePay is True, then this is the value used to calculate the employer pension contributions

automaticAWECalculation.description:
If set to True then we'll automatically calculate the AverageWeeklyEarnings. 
Set it to false if you want to manually provide a figure that overrides our calculations

averageWeeklyEarnings.description:
The employees average weekly earnings. Only relevant for Statutory Pay
It's advised that you don't try to calculate this yourself.

babyDate.description:
Only required for Parental Leave with Statutory Pay
If Type is Maternity or Paternity then this is the date the baby is due.
For Adoption this is the Matching Date.
For Neonatal Care is the Baby Due / Matching / Placement Date

customPayments.description:
If this is Sick Leave with Custom Payment, then this stores the RoleId, PercentOfPay and PaySSPInAddition for each
role assigned to an employee that did not end before the start date of the absence.

customProRataRules.description:
If this is a Leave that supports changing the pro rata at leave level,
This stores the RoleId and ProRataMethod for each
role assigned to an employee that did not end before the start date of the absence.

documentCount.description: [readonly] The number of attachments associated with this model

documents.description: [readonly] The attachments associated with this model

externalId.description: If the Leave comes from an ExternalDataProvider, then this is its Id in the ExternalDataProvider

from.description:
The first day of Leave.
If it's a half day PM then set the time portion to 12:00:00, otherwise leave it blank or set it to 00:00:00

historicOmpRequiresProcessing.description:
Only used during the creation and update of occupational maternity leaves.
When creating or updating historical occupational leaves, this will determine whether to pay the historic payments or correction in the next PayRun.

historicOspRequiresProcessing.description:
Only used during the creation of sickness Occupational Policy.
When creating historical SOP, this will determine whether to pay that leave in the next PayRun.

historicSmpRequiresProcessing.description:
Only used during the creation and update of statutory maternity leaves.
When creating or updating historical statutory maternity leaves, this will determine whether to pay the historic payments or correction in the next PayRun.

historicSspRequiresProcessing.description:
Only used during the creation of historical SSP.
When creating historical SSP, this will determine whether to pay that leave in the next PayRun.

historicSxpRequiresProcessing.description: Used during to determine whether to back pay before current payrun

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isReversed.description:
If the reversal of this Leave has already been processed,
this will be set to true

kitSplitDays.description: If the LeaveType supports KIT/SPLIT days then use this property to store the list of dates

lastProcessedFrom.description:
[readonly] Last Processed From Date
If this Leave has already been processed,
this will be the original leave from date

notes.description: A free-form text field to record any comments

occupationalMaternityPolicyUniqueId.description: Occupational Maternity Policy Id when leave type is Maternity and payment type is occupational policy

offsetPay.description:
If this Leave has Statutory Pay  and this is set to True and the employe eis paid a fixed amoutn per period
with Leave Adjustments set to automatic, then we'll reduce their pay for the period by the statutory amount
so the employee still gets paid the full amount.

openingOmpPay.description: Opening occupational pay which has already been paid to the employee

openingPay.description: Opening pay which has already been paid to the employee in another system

overridePaymentDescription.description: If Pay is StatutoryPay and you want to override our description that goes with the payment then set this to true

overridenPaymentDescription.description: If OverridePaymentDescription is true and Pay is set to StatutoryPay then we'll use this as the description for the payment amount.

payRunExistsWithOccMaternityPay.description: Indicates whether there is occupational maternity payment associated with a leave in the current pay run.

secondaryBabyDate.description:
Only used for Parental Leave with Statutory Pay
If Type is Maternity, Paternity, SharedParental (Birth) then this is the the Baby Born Date.
For Adoption or SharedParental (Adoption) this is the Expected Placement Date.

sspPayFromDayOne.description:
If this is Sick Leave with Statutory Pay then setting this to true
will force SSP to be paid from day one rather than the usual rule 
of the first Working Day after 3 Qualifying Days

strikeHoursToDeduct.description:
The number of hours to be deducted at the employee's contractual rate
This property is valid for strike deducted hours

tertiaryBabyDate.description:
Only used for Parental Leave with Statutory Pay
If Type is Adoption this is the Placement Date.
If Type is Neonatal Care is the Date Baby Entered Neonatal Care

to.description:
The last day of Leave.
If it's a half day AM then set the time portion to 11:59:59, otherwise set it to 23:59:59

totalDays.description:
[readonly] The number of days covered by this leave, regardless of whether or not they're working days.
This is calculated based on the employees Working Pattern.

totalDaysOverride.description: If a value is provided here then this will be used in place of the calculated TotalDays value

useAssumedPensionablePay.description:
If this Leave has Statutory Pay then if this is set to True
we will use the value set in AssumedPensionablePay to work out the employer pension contributions

useOpeningPay.description: Use the OpeningPay which has already been paid in another system

workingDays.description:
[readonly] The number of working days covered by this leave.
This is calculated based on the employees Working Pattern.

workingDaysOverride.description: If a value is provided here then this will be used in place of the calculated WorkingDays value

assumedPensionablePay.description:
Assumed Pensionable Pay (APP) is an average figure, calculated as the average pay in the 3 months
(or 12 weeks if weekly/fortnightly/fourweekly paid) before the absence

automaticAPPCalculation.description:
If set to True then we'll automatically calculate the AssumedPensionablePay. 
Set it to false if you want to manually provide a figure that overrides our calculations

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isPrimary.description: Boolean flag indicates if the role is primary

paySSPInAddition.description:
If this is Sick Leave with Custom Payment,
If true, then reduce the OSP by the amount of SSP paid (and apportioned according to role weighting)
If false, then do not reduce the OSP at all.

percentOfPay.description:
If this is Sick Leave with Custom Payment,
the user will input the desired impact on the employee’s pay, per role.
This will be via an amount between 0-100 in 'Pay for Absence'.
The employees pay will be fully reduced for the absence, and the % paid will be added back using the OSP pay code.

accrueHoursPerDay.description: If HolidayType is Accrual_Days then this value is used to help convert hours worked into days accrued

accruePaymentInLieuAllGrossPay.description: Set to true if you want accrued holiday payments to be calculated on the total gross pay for the employee or just on the single regular pay element

accruePaymentInLieuPayAutomatically.description: Set to true if you want employees to be automatically paid any outstanding holiday pay

accruePaymentInLieuRate.description: The rate at which Payments in Lieu acrrue. Typically this should be 12.07%.

accrueSetAmount.description: If true and HolidayType is Accrual_Days then the AccruePaymentInLieuRate will be treated as the set amount to accrue per period worked.

accruedHoursAdjustment.description: Any manual adjustment to the total hours accrued

accruedHoursAmount.description: [readonly] The total accrued hours for this employee over the lifetime of their employment so far

accruedHoursAmountLastPeriod.description: [readonly] The total accrued hours for this employee in the last pay period

accruedHoursBalance.description: [readonly] The balance of hours owed to this employee in lieu of holidays

accruedHoursPaid.description: [readonly] The Total amount of hours paid to this employee in lieu of holidays

accruedPaymentAdjustment.description: Any manual adjustment to the total accrued

accruedPaymentBalance.description: [readonly] The balance of what is owed to this employee in lieu of holidays

accruedPaymentLiability.description: [readonly] The total accrued payments for this employee over the lifetime of their employment so far

accruedPaymentPaid.description: [readonly] The Total amount paid to this employee in lieu of holidays

adjustment.description:
Adjustment to number of hours/days/weeks holiday this employee can take per year.
Will reset to 0 when the Allowance resets.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

allowance.description:
The number of days holiday an employee can take per year if HolidayType is Days.
Otherwise this is readonly and gives you the number of days accrued since the last reset

allowanceRemaining.description: [readonly] The number of days remaining of the allowance until next reset

allowanceResetDate.description: The date that the holiday allowance resets. Only the day/month part of the value is relevant.

allowanceUsed.description: [readonly] The number of days used from the allowance since last reset

allowanceUsedPreviousPeriod.description: [readonly] The number of days used in the 12 months prior to the last reset

holidayAccrualResetDate.description: [readonly] This date is used to reset holiday accrual schemes (Accrual Money or Accrual Hours).

maximumAccruePeriod.description: The maximum number of hours capable of being accrued in a single period

paidHoursAmountLastPeriod.description: [readonly] The number of hours paid against the pay code

showAhpOnPayslip.description: If true then the AHP balance will be shown on the employees payslip.

showAllowanceOnPayslip.description: If true then the remaining Allowance will be shown on the employees payslip.

useDefaultAccrueHoursPerDay.description:
If true then the value for AccrueHoursPerDay comes from the Employer record.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

useDefaultAccruePaymentInLieu.description:
If true then the value for AccruePaymentInLieu comes from the Employer record.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

useDefaultAccruePaymentInLieuPayAutomati.description:
If true then the value for AccruePaymentInLieu comes from the Employer record.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

useDefaultAccruePaymentInLieuRate.description:
If true then the value for AccruePaymentInLieuRate comes from the Employer record.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

useDefaultAllowance.description:
If true then the value for the Allowance comes from the Employer record.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings if a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

useDefaultAllowanceResetDate.description:
If true then the value for the AllowanceResetDate comes from the Employer record.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

useDefaultHolidayAccrualBasis.description:
If true then the value for HolidayAccrualBasis comes from the Employer record.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

useDefaultHolidayAccrualDefaultRateType.description: If true then the value for HolidayAccrualDefaultRateType comes from the Employer record.

useDefaultHolidayType.description:
If true then the value for HolidayType comes from the Employer record.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

useDefaultMaximumAccruePeriod.description:
If true then the value for MaximumAccruePeriod comes from the Employer record.
This property only appears if the LeaveSettings is a child of an Employee (not of an Employer)

LinkedPiw.description:
Linked Period of Incapacity for Work.
If you record Sick Leave and select Statutory Pay then any other Sick Leave with Statutory Pay
lasting 4 or more days in the previous 8 weeks will be linked to it

averageWeeklyEarnings.description: [readonly] Average weekly earnings from linked Leave

id.description: [readonly] The Id of the linked Leave

sspFirstDay.description: [readonly] The SspFirstDay from the linked Leave, if set

sspFirstPayDay.description: [readonly] The SspFirstPayDay from the linked Leave, if set

totalDays.description: [readonly] The number of days covered by the linked Leave.

workingDays.description: [readonly] The number of working days in the linked Leave.

Loan.description: Used to represent an Attachment of Earnings Order (AEO)

amountRepaid.description: [readonly] The amount repaid so far due to deductions in Payruns

documentCount.description: [readonly] The number of attachments associated with this model

documents.description: [readonly] The attachments associated with this model

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isPaused.description: If set to true then deductions will not be made

issueDate.description: The date the Loan was made

loanAmount.description: The initial amount that the loan was for

payCode.description:
The Code of the PayCode used for recording payments.
The PayCode must have a CalculationType of FixedAmount and not be a multiplier code

periodAmount.description: The amount to deduct per PayPeriod

previouslyPaid.description: [readonly] Any amount by which to adjust the Balance, perhaps due to repayments made elsewhere

reference.description: An optional reference for this Loan

LoanCharge.description:
Part of the TaxYearConfig that our engine uses to calculate charges for a Loan.
It is used internally when our engine performs calculations.
You do not need to do anything with this model, it's provided purely for informational purposes.

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

paySpineId.description: foreign key with pay spine table

MailSettings.description:
Determines the settings used when the Employer sends emails.
If CustomiseSmtpSettings is false then SmtpSettings will be null and our default internal settings will be used;

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

senderEmailVerified.description: [readonly] The SenderEmail must be verified before you can send email using that address

useSystemFromAddress.description:
If set to true then the SenderEmail provided will only be used in the 
ReplyTo fields. The system defaults will be used for the From address.

MileageAllowancePaymentsRate.description:
Part of the TaxYearConfig that our engine uses to calculate Mileage Allowance Payments.
It is used internally when our engine performs calculations.
You do not need to do anything with this model, it's provided purely for informational purposes.

billPastEndDate.description: If set to true then this user should still be billed even after the End Date

NationalInsuranceCalculation.description:
Included as part of the PayRunEntry model to provide details of how the National Insurance Contribution was calculated.
Unless the PayRunEntry.ManualNi property is set to true then these value will all be read-only and are recalculated everytime a payrun is updated.
This calculation could be made up of one or more calculations made on different NI table letters.
Where more than NI table letter affects the calculation, the calculation for each NI table letter will be contain in the Breakdown.

breakdown.description: [readonly] List of calculations that make up this calculation

earningsAboveFUSTUptoIncludingUEL.description: Earnings above FUST up to UEL

earningsAboveLELUptoIncludingPT.description: Earnings above LEL up to PT

earningsAbovePTUptoIncludingST.description: Earnings above PT up to ST

earningsAbovePTUptoIncludingUEL.description: Earnings above PT up to UEL

earningsAboveSTUptoIncludingFUST.description: Earnings above ST up to FUST

earningsAboveSTUptoIncludingUEL.description: Earnings above ST up to UEL

earningsUptoIncludingLEL.description: Earnings up to and including LEL

niablePay.description: Niable pay value, required for payrun overrides

NationalInsuranceCalculationBase.description:
Included as part of the PayRunEntry model to provide details of how the National Insurance Contribution was calculated.
Unless the PayRunEntry.ManualNi property is set to true then these value will all be read-only and are recalculated everytime a payrun is updated.
This calculation could be made up of one or more calculations made on different NI table letters.
Where more than NI table letter affects the calculation, the calculation for each NI table letter will be contain in the Breakdown.

earningsAboveFUSTUptoIncludingUEL.description: Earnings above FUST up to UEL

earningsAboveLELUptoIncludingPT.description: Earnings above LEL up to PT

earningsAbovePTUptoIncludingST.description: Earnings above PT up to ST

earningsAbovePTUptoIncludingUEL.description: Earnings above PT up to UEL

earningsAboveSTUptoIncludingFUST.description: Earnings above ST up to FUST

earningsAboveSTUptoIncludingUEL.description: Earnings above ST up to UEL

earningsUptoIncludingLEL.description: Earnings up to and including LEL

niablePay.description: Niable pay value, required for payrun overrides

NationalInsuranceCalculationPeriodValues.description:
Part of the TaxYearConfig that our engine uses to calculate National Insurance Contributions.
It is used internally when our engine performs calculations.
You do not need to do anything with this model, it's provided purely for informational purposes.

NationalInsuranceCode.description:
Part of the TaxYearConfig that our engine uses to calculate National Insurance Contributions.
It is used internally when our engine performs calculations.
You do not need to do anything with this model, it's provided purely for informational purposes.

description.description: [readonly] Description of Employees that would use this NI Letter

eeB.description: [readonly] Earnings at or above LEL up to and including PT (Employee Contribution)

eeC.description: [readonly] Earnings above the PT up to and including UEL (Employee Contribution)

eeD.description: [readonly] Balance of earnings above UEL (Employee Contribution)

effectiveFrom.description: The date when these period values are effective

erB.description: [readonly] Earnings at or above LEL up to and including PT (Employer Contribution)

erC.description: [readonly]  Earnings above the PT up to and including UEL (Employer Contribution)

erD.description: [readonly] Balance of earnings above UEL (Employer Contribution)

midYearChanges.description: Any optional changes to the period values that are effective from a specified date in the middle of a tax year

NationalMinimumWage.description:
Part of the TaxYearConfig that our engine uses to calculate National Minimum Wage.
It is used internally when our engine performs calculations.
You do not need to do anything with this model, it's provided purely for informational purposes.

basedOnPayrun.description: If false, then any payrun related information (Tax year, etc) should be ignored.

isLatestVersion.description: [readonly] Indicates whether this is the latest version of the NicSummary.

payRunVersion.description: [readonly] The supplementary pay run version of this NicSummary.

externalId.description:
If you will be using the API to post journals to an ExternalDataProvider, then this is the Id of the nominal code in the external system.
If there's no mapping then this will have a value of "0"

Note.description: Used to represent a Note for an Employee

xml.description:
This property will soon be removed and should not be used.
There is now a dedicated API endpoint for retrieving the XML for a submission.

daysOrWeeksBeforeRecalculatingEntitlemen.description: How many days or weeks an employee needs to return to work before new entitlements are used in calculations

ignorePreviousYearsSickness.description:
Defines whether any sickness that relates to a previous year's sickness entitlement should be included in the entitlement calculation,
or whether only sickness that is relevant to the current year's entitlement is taken into consideration

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isReversed.description: [readonly] Is set to true if this has been reversed in a Leave

P11.description:
A P11 Report summarises payments and deductions made to an employee.
Our Reports API can return this to you in many formats including as a PDF file
If you request is as a JSOn object then it is represented using this model.

P11Detailed.description:
A more comprehensive P11 Report.
Our Reports API can return this to you in several formats including as a PDF file
If you request a JSON object then it is represented using this model.

P11NiValues.description: Forms a part of the P11 report.

PapdisDocument.description:
PAPDIS stands for 'Payroll and Pension Data Interface Standard'.
It is an industry standard for exchanging data between payroll software and pension providers.
Our system provides an export of data in this standard and these models are used to represent the data.
Whilst the relevant reporting API endpoint can provide this data as a JSON entity, it is usually represented in CSV or XML format which our API also provides.

Used to encapsulate a response for any of the reports.
