hasPrevioulyLeftPension.description: Flag indicating the employee has previously left the pension

id.description: [readonly]

isPreviousTaxYearRefund.description: Indicates whether the employee has a pension refund from the previous tax year.

lastPaymentDate.description: The last time the employee was paid

totalPreviousTaxYearRefundEmployeeAmount.description:
The total amount of pension refunds paid to the employee,
calculated as the sum of all EmployeeRefund values from applicable PensionRefunds (when IsPreviousTaxYearRefund is enabled).

totalPreviousTaxYearRefundEmployeeAvcAmo.description:
The total amount of AVC (Additional Voluntary Contributions) refunds paid to the employee,
calculated as the sum of all AdditionalVoluntaryContribution values from applicable PensionRefunds (when IsPreviousTaxYearRefund is enabled).

totalPreviousTaxYearRefundEmployerAmount.description:
The total amount of employer pension refunds,
calculated as the sum of all EmployerRefund values from applicable PensionRefunds (when IsPreviousTaxYearRefund is enabled).

isPensionAvcPayLine.description: Indicates whether the pay run entry includes a pension AVC pay line.

employerId.description: [readonly] Taken from the papdisEmployerId property of the PensionProvider

group.description: [readonly] Taken from the papdisGroup property of the WorkerGroup

id.description: [readonly]

subGroup.description: [readonly] Taken from the papdisSubGroup property of the WorkerGroup

employees.description: [readonly] Employees and contributions made in this period

pensionProviderId.description: [readonly] Taken from the papdisProviderId property of the PensionProvider

PayCode.description:
Each PayLine has a Code. The Code will match the Code property of a PayCode.
The PayCode that is used determines how the amount is treated with regards to tax, NI and pensions

dailyRateMultiplier.description: If the MultiplierType is MultipleOfDailyRate then this sets the multiple to be used, ie 1.5

defaultValue.description: If a non-zero value is provided then when this code is selected in our UI the value will be automatically filled.

hourlyRateMultiplier.description: If the MultiplierType is MultipleOfHourlyRate then this sets the multiple to be used, ie 1.5

isAttachable.description: Determines whether or not the value of PayLines using this code should be taken into consideration when calculating the attachable pay for AttachmentOrders.

isAutoAdjust.description: Can be used to inform a User to what default value to set for PayLine.autoAdjustForLeave for new lines created.

isControlCode.description: [readonly] Control Codes cannot be deleted or edited nor can PayLines be assigned to them.

isDeduction.description: If set to true then a PayLine using this code will be treated as a deduction as opposed to an addition.

isNiable.description: Determines whether or not the value of PayLines using this code should be taken into consideration when calculating National Insurance Contributions.

isNotContributingToHolidayPay.description: If true then any payments made using this code will not contribute towards holiday pay calculations.

isNotTierable.description: If true then any payments made using this code will not be included when calculating the tier an employee should be on for a Tiered Pension.

isNotional.description: Determines whether the transaction involves non-monetary benefits for payroll processing, potentially subject to tax and National Insurance.

isPensionExcessLumpSum.description:
If true then any payments made using this code will be reported as
a Lump Sums (B - PCELS)

isPensionable.description: Determines whether or not the value of PayLines using this code should be taken into consideration when calculating the total pensionable pay.

isQualifyingEarningsForAe.description:
If true then any payments made using this code will be used as the basis for calculating QualifyingEarnings for AutoEnrolment assessments.
If it is set to null then when it's next retrieved it'll have the same value as PensionablePay

isRealTimeClass1aNiable.description: Determines whether or not the value of PayLines using this code are subject to real time Class 1A NIC.

isStandAloneLumpSum.description:
If true then any payments made using this code will be reported as
a Lump Sums (A - SALS)

isSystemCode.description: [readonly] System Codes cannot be deleted or edited

isTaxable.description: Determines whether or not the value of PayLines using this code should be taken into consideration when calculating the PAYE liability.

isTcp_Op.description:
If true then any payments made using this code will be reported as
a Trivial Commutation Payment (C - Occupational Pension)

isTcp_Pp.description:
If true then any payments made using this code will be reported as
a Trivial Commutation Payment (B - Personal Pension)

isTcp_Tcls.description:
If true then any payments made using this code will be reported as
a Trivial Commutation Payment (A - TCLS)

payee.description: The Id of the Payee, if any, that deductions are to be paid to.

PayCodeSet.description: A PayCodeSet is used to group together a number of PayCodes.

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

payCodes.description: The Code of any PayCodes included in this PayCodeSet

useForLgps.description: Flag indicating if this PayCodeSet will be used for LGPS pay categorisation

PayElementReportReportResponse.description:
Used to encapsulate a response for any of the reports.
See the Introduction Guide for Reports for more details

content.description: [readonly] This could contain a link to a PDF file, HTML content or other content, depending on the Type value.

type.description: [readonly] The content-type, this would usually be the same as the accept header you provided when you requested the report

PayGradesReportReportResponse.description:
Used to encapsulate a response for any of the reports.
See the Introduction Guide for Reports for more details

content.description: [readonly] This could contain a link to a PDF file, HTML content or other content, depending on the Type value.

type.description: [readonly] The content-type, this would usually be the same as the accept header you provided when you requested the report

PayLine.description:
As well as the basic pay amount for an employee there are often additions and deductions such as bonuses.
These additions and deductions are represented using this model.

analysisCategoryCodeIds.description: List of analysis Category Codes  uniqueId

annualValue.description:
The original value from the PayLine before we modify it
Currently only used when CalculationType is set to FixedAnnualAmount
so we can determine whether the pay line value needs calculating or not
To recalculate set to null with annual amount recorded in Value

attachmentOrderId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated AttachmentOrder. Only included if the Code is AEO

calculateAsWhenPaid.description: Indicates whether this pay line should be treated as When Paid rather than When Earned

childId.description: This is nothing but the UniqueId of the model.

code.description: The Code of the PayCode this line is assigned to. The PayCode determines the treatment of this line when it comes to NI, Tax and Pensions as well as whether it's a deduction or addition.

contributesToBasicPay.description:
Indicates whether this pay line contributes to the basic pay of the employee.
Will be set to true by default when IsAutoGeneratedBasicPayLine is true.

description.description: A free form description to accompany this line. It will be displayed on the payslip.

earnedFrom.description: Nullable DateTime property "EarnedFrom" in the PayLine model to store the start date of the PayLine's 'earned' period in a PayRun.

earnedTo.description: Nullable DateTime property "EarnedTo" in the PayLine model to store the end date of the PayLine's 'earned' period in a PayRun.

effectiveFrom.description: The date the pay line starts being calculated in payrun

effectiveTo.description: The date the pay line stops being calculated in payrun

fullTimeAnnualValue.description: Used to set Full Time Annual Value for payline

ignoreInitialBackPay.description:
Skip the automatic backpay calculation on the first pay run, if the addition/deduction had been paid outside or Staffology.
If set to false, the automatic backpay calculation will be performed on the first pay run.

initialBackPayPublicPayRunId.description: Used to mark the first payrun where this payline has been payrolled for backpay

isAutomaticBackPay.description:
Automatically calculate back pay and add or modify the necessary pay lines for this addition/deduction when a pay run is created.
Applicable only to employee level addition/deduction pay lines.
If set to false then you must manually handle the calculation and adding of back pay lines.

isNetToGross.description: If the PayLine is a fixed amount addition without multiplier then this property may be set to true so that the amount of the addition to be considered a take home pay target.

isZeroHoursTeachersBackPayLine.description:
[readonly] Indicates whether this pay line is a backdated pay claim line
belonging to a zero hours contract teacher.

leaveId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated Leave. Only included if the PayLine is a result of a Leave with Statutory pay

leaveStatutoryDaysPaid.description: [readonly] If the PayLine is a result of a Leave with Statutory Pay then this property tells you how many days they've been paid for (based on their Working Pattern).

leaveStatutoryWeeksPaid.description: [readonly] If the PayLine is a result of a Leave with Statutory Pay then this property tells you how many weeks they've been paid for (based on their Working Pattern).

loanId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated Loan, if any.

londonAllowanceId.description: Used to set London allowance id for payline

multiplier.description: If the related PayCode has a MultiplierType other than None then this field will be used as number of days or hours

netToGrossDiscrepancy.description: The discrepancy between the targeted and the calculated grossed up value during a net to gross calculation.

parentId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated employee level pay line, if the current line is a back pay line.

paySpineGradeId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated pay spine grade.

paySpineId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated pay spine.

pensionId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated Pension. Only included if the Code is PENSION, PENSIONSS or PENSIONRAS

percentageOfEffectiveDays.description: The percentage of working days the effective dates span for pro-rata'ing values, calculated/set during each payrun

periodFrom.description: Nullable DateTime property "PeriodFrom" in the PayLine model to store the start date of the PayRun period the current PayLine is part of.

periodTo.description: Nullable DateTime property "PeriodTo" in the PayLine model to store the end date of the PayRun period the current PayLine is part of.

rate.description: If the related  PayCode has a MultiplierType other than None then this field will be used as the rate per day or hour. Otherwise it isn't used

roleId.description:
The employee role which is associated with this pay line.
If set to null, the pay line is not related with a role.

spinalPointId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated spinal point.

targetNetToGrossValue.description: The original net fixed addition amount that is considered to be a take home pay target.

value.description:
The amount to add or deduct (whether it is a deduction or addition depends on the PayCode used).
If the PayCode has a CalculationType other than FixedAmount then this field will be a percentage.
If the PayCode has a MultiplierType other than None then this field will be readonly and automatically calculated.

PayOptions.description: This object forms the basis of the Employees payment.

baseDailyRate.description:
This property is used to calculate values for PayCodes that are set as multiples of 
the employees base daily rate. Eg sick.
If this is set as zero then we'll attempt to calculate a value based on the other fields

baseHourlyRate.description:
This property is used to calculate values for PayCodes that are set as multiples of 
the employees base hourly rate. Eg Overtime.
If this is set as zero then we'll attempt to calculate a value based on the other fields

basicPay.description:
The amount of basic pay the Employee is regularly paid each period
This amount is the combined total of all pay elements that contribute to basic pay

excludedRegularPayLines.description: These used to store the PayLines that have been excluded from RegularPaylines.

mapsMiles.description: The number of miles to pay for as Mileage Allowance Payments

ordinal.description: Indicates whether to use first, second, third (etc) PaySchedule for this PayPeriod.

payAmount.description: The amount the Employee is regularly paid each period

payAmountMultiplier.description:
This property is irrelevant if the basis is Monthly.
But if the basis is Daily or Hourly then this property sets how many days/hours the employee should be paid for in the period.

payCode.description: If you want the Employees pay to be allocated to a code other than BASIC, specify it here

regularPayLines.description:
These are used to make additions/deductions to the pay for this Employee.
You do not need to include Pension, Tax, NI,  Loan Repayments, etc as these will all be automatically created.

withholdTaxRefundIfPayIsZero.description: If you don't want negative PAYE values when the Gross pay is zero, set this to true

PayOptionsImport.description: This object is used to import payment information for a payrun entry

analysisCategoryCodes.description: List of Analysis Category Codes associated with this item on the payslip.

costCentre.description: Any CostCentre that you'd like to appear on the payslip

department.description: Any Department that you'd like to appear on the payslip

effectiveFrom.description: EffectiveFrom date for imported pay lines

effectiveTo.description: EffectiveTo date for imported pay lines

employerIdentifier.description:
Optional. But if one entry has it then all must.
Allows you to import to multiple employers by specifying the Employers AlternativeIdentifier

note.description: Any note that you'd like to appear on the payslip

payAmount.description: The amount the Employee is regularly paid each period

payAmountMultiplier.description:
This property is irrelevant if the basis is Monthly.
But if the basis is Daily or Hourly then this property sets how many days/hours the employee should be paid for in the period.

payCode.description: If you want to override the PayCode used for the Basic Pay then set the code here, otherwise leave this blank and the default will be used.

payrollCode.description: The payroll code of the employee to update

roleReference.description: This is the unique reference of the Role that the employee is assigned to.

PayPeriodEventType.description: Different events supported for a PaySchedule Period. These events happen a pre-configured number of days before the PaymentDate for that PaySchedulePeriod.

PayRun.description:
This model is right at the very heart of the software.
There is a PayRun for each period in which people are paid.

autoPilotCloseDate.description:
[readonly] If AutoPilot is enabled in the AutomationSettings for the Employer then this property will tell you when
the payrun will be automatically closed

description.description: Description of the current version of the PayRun.

employeeCount.description: [readonly] The number of Employees included in this PayRun (including any CIS Subcontractors)

endDate.description: [readonly] The end date of the period this PayRun covers

entries.description:
[readonly] The PayRunEntries that make up this PayRun.
This is populate automatically when you start a PayRun.

isClosed.description: [readonly] Set to True if the PayRun is Finalised and changes can no longer be made

isCombinedPayRun.description: Indicates if the payRun is a combined pay run or not

isLatestVersion.description: [readonly] Indicates whether this is the latest version of the PayRun.

isRolledBack.description: [readonly] Set to True if the PayRun is currently rolled back

nonRtiEmployeeCount.description: [readonly] The number of employees excluded from the RTI submission in this PayRun

ordinal.description: [readonly] Indicates whether this uses first, second, third (etc.) PaySchedule for this PayPeriod.

originalPayRunPaymentDate.description: [readonly] If this PayRun is a supplementary PayRun then this will be the date of the original PayRun's Payment Date

paymentDate.description: [readonly] The intended date that Employees will be paid, although this can be changed on a per PayRunEntry basis

payslipScheduledDateTime.description: The scheduled date time for sending payslips by email.

period.description: [readonly] The period (i.e, Tax Week or Tax Month) that this PayRun is for.

startDate.description: [readonly] The start date of the period this PayRun covers

subContractorCount.description: [readonly] The number of CIS Subcontractors included in this PayRun

taxMonth.description: [readonly] The Tax Month that the Payment Date falls in

version.description: [readonly] The version of this PayRun in case any supplementary pay runs have been created for the same period.

payRunEntries.description: A list of PayRunEntries where the PayRunEntry itself has been modified and.or the related Employee record was changed

PayRunEntry.description:
A PayRun contains multiple PayRunEntries, one for each Employee that is being paid.
The value for ```PersonalDetails``` and ```EmploymentDetails``` is copied from the Employee record.
So to change them you should update the Employee, not the PayRunEntry.
Changes made to the Employee are only reflected whilst the PayRun is still open.
Once you finalise and close the PayRun then a snapshot is taken of these values which is stored with the PayRunEntry for future reference.

aeNotEnroledWarning.description: [readonly] If true then this Employee needs to be on an Auto Enrolment pension but isn't yet.

bacsHashCode.description: [readonly] A Hash Code used for RTI BACS Hash Cross Reference

bacsSubReference.description: [readonly] A randomly generated string for use with the RTI Hash Cross Reference

differencePensionSummaries.description: [readonly] A summary of the details for the Pensions (if any) that the Employee is assigned to.

emailId.description:
If the Payslip for this PayRunEntry has been emailed to the employee then the Id for an EmployerEmail will be provided here.
if the value is all zeroes then the email is in the process of being created.

employeeRolePayItems.description:
[readonly] Automatically populated.
Array of base hourly and daily rates for the employee roles

forcedCisVatAmount.description:
If this employee is a CIS Subcontractor registered for VAT, we'll automatically work out VAT at the set rate.
If you want to override this calculations then set this property to anything other than null.

hasAttachmentOrders.description: [readonly] Indicates that there are AttachmentOrders for this Employee in this entry

holidayAccrued.description: The amount of holiday days or hours accrued in the period.

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isClosed.description: [readonly] Set to True if the PayRun is Finalised and changes can no longer be made to the PayRunEntries

isLatestVersion.description: [readonly] Indicates whether this is the latest version of the PayRunEntry.

isNewStarter.description: Determines whether or not this Employee will be declared as a new starter on the resulting FPS

isRemoved.description: Has the entry been removed from the payrun

isRolledBack.description: [readonly] Set to True if the Pay Run Entry is currently rolled back

manualNi.description:
If set to true then you must provide your own value for NationalInsuranceCalculation.
You'd normally leave this set to false and let us automatically calculate NI amounts.

niSplit.description: If set to true Ni calculations are done for backdated pay for each pay period separately and then summed up

note.description: Any note that you'd like to appear on the payslip

ordinal.description: [readonly] Indicates whether this uses the first, second, third (etc) PaySchedule for this PayPeriod.

originalPayRunEntryPaymentDate.description: [readonly] If this PayRunEntry is a supplementary PayRunEntry then this will be the date of the original PayRunEntry's Payment Date

payRunVersion.description: [readonly] The supplementary pay run version of this PayRunEntry.

paymentAfterLeaving.description: [readonly] If this payment is for an employee that has left then this is set to true.

paymentDate.description: The date this payment was or will be made

payrollCodeChanged.description: [readonly] Indicates whether or not the Payroll Code for this Employee has changed since the last FPS

pensionSummaries.description: [readonly] A summary of the details for the Pensions (if any) that the Employee is assigned to.

percentageOfWorkingDaysPaidAsNormal.description:
[readonly] If the employee is paid a set amount per period (ie, not an hourly or daily rate) and there is any Leave that is either Not Paid or Statutory Pay then this value
will give the percentage of working days (based on the Working Pattern) that should be paid as normal.
If there is no Leave in the period or PayOptions.AutoAdjustForLeave is false, then this will be 1.

period.description: [readonly] The Tax Week or Tax Month number this PayRunEntry relates to

periodOverrides.description: Any calculated values for this period that should be overridden with a different value

periodsCovered.description: [readonly] The amount of periods that the Pay Run Entry covers. This can be > 1 when back pay for previous periods is paid in the current period

priorPayrollCode.description:
[readonly] If the FPS needs to declare a change of PayId then this will contain the previous code
It's worked out automatically but can also be set from the Employees EmploymentDetails property.

recievingOffsetPay.description:
If the pay is being topped up due to an applied Leave having the offset value set to true then
this will be set to true

taxMonth.description: [readonly] The Tax Month that the Payment Date falls in

totalsYtdOverrides.description: Any values of TotalsYtd that should be overridden with a different value

unpaidAbsence.description: [readonly] Indicates that there was unpaid absence in the pay period

workingDaysNotPaidAsNormal.description:
[readonly] If PercentageOfWorkingDaysPaidAsNormal has a value other than 1
then this property will tell you how many working days have been deducted from the basic pay
due to either being Not Paid or Statutory Pay

link.description: [readonly] If available, a link to the journal in the ExternalDataProvider

statusMessage.description: [readonly] A message to elaborate on the Status

title.description: [readonly] The status of the task for posting this journal to an ExternalDataProvider

bacsHash.description: [readonly] if paying by Credit and you've enabled BacsHash then this will contain the BacsHash included on the FPS

bacsSubReference.description: [readonly] if paying by Credit and you've enabled BacsHash then this will contain the BacsSubReference included on the FPS

date.description: [readonly] The date the payment is to be made

employerPaymentReference.description: The employer's payment reference, formatted as per the employer settings

isBankDetailValid.description: [readonly] for checking if bank details are valid

reference.description: [readonly] The period the payment is for

employeeUniqueIds.description:
List of employee unique ids, whose PayRunEntries to be re-opened during a Payrun rollback operation.
Will be used only during a transition to a rolled back pay run state

reasonText.description: A free-form text field for a reason for the change of state.

PayRunSummaryLine.description:
An array of this model is used to represent a summary of a PayRun.
This would typically be used for creating accounting entries for the PayRun.

analysisCategories.description: [readonly] If the journal is split by analysis categories

analysisCategoryCodes.description: [readonly] If the journal is split by analysis categories then the relevant analysis categories code is shown here.

costCentreCode.description: [readonly] If the journal is split by cost centre then the relevant cost centre code is shown here.

departmentCode.description: [readonly] If the journal is split by department then the relevant department code is shown here.

description.description: [readonly] A description of what this line summarises.

employeeUniqueId.description: [readonly] The EmployeeId this summary line relates to.

journalNarrative.description: [readonly] Gets the journal narrative for the pension, if defined.

nominalCode.description: [readonly] If you've set up NominalCodeMapping then the relevant Nominal code for the PayCode will be shown here.

pensionId.description: [readonly] The PensionId of the pension associated with this summary line, only present for pension summary lines.

qty.description: [readonly] If the PayCode is a Multiplier code then this will contain the number of days/hours

unMappedNominalCode.description: [readonly] Unmapped Nominal Code is the Nominal Code without the Nominal Mapping.

value.description: [readonly] The total value for this line.

PayRunSummaryLineIEnumerableReportRespon.description:
Used to encapsulate a response for any of the reports.
See the Introduction Guide for Reports for more details

content.description: [readonly] This could contain a link to a PDF file, HTML content or other content, depending on the Type value.

model.description: [readonly] If the type is application.json then this will contain a JSON representation of the relevant model

type.description: [readonly] The content-type, this would usually be the same as the accept header you provided when you requested the report

PayRunTotals.description:
Used to represent totals for a PayRun or PayRunEntry.
If a value is 0 then it will not be shown in the JSON.

additions.description: The value of all additions.  This minus Deductions should equal TakeHomePay

adjustments.description: [readonly] The value of adjustments made to the Net Pay (Non taxable/NIable additions/deductions)

appLevyDeduction.description: [readonly] Value of any Apprenticeship Levy fee deducted for processing the umbrella payment

attachmentOrderDeductions.description: [readonly] Value of deductions made due to AttachmentOrders

basicPay.description: [readonly] The amount to be paid to this Employee as a result of the PayOptions that have been set

cisDeduction.description: [readonly] Value of any CIS Deduction made

cisUmbrellaFee.description: [readonly] Value of any pre-tax fee charged to the CIS Subcontractor for processing the payment

cisUmbrellaFeePostTax.description: [readonly] Value of any post-tax fee charged to the CIS Subcontractor for processing the payment

cisVat.description: [readonly] Value of any VAT paid to CIS Subcontractor

dednsFromNetPay.description: [readonly] The value of any deductions being made to the Net Pay for this Employee

deductions.description: The value of all deductions.  Additions minus This value should equal TakeHomePay

empeePenContribnsNotPaid.description: [readonly] Value of employee pension contributions that are not paid under 'net pay arrangements', including any AVC

empeePenContribnsPaid.description: [readonly] Value of employee pension contributions paid under 'net pay arrangements', including any AVC

employeePensionContribution.description: [readonly] The value of the Pension Contribution being made by this Employee, excluding any Additional Voluntary Contributions

employeePensionContributionAvc.description: [readonly] The value of the Pension Contribution being made by this Employee as an Additional Voluntary Contribution

employerNiOffPayroll.description: [readonly] The amount included in EmployerNi that is in relation to Off-Payroll Workers

employerPensionContribution.description: [readonly] The value of the Pension Contribution being made by the Employer for this Employee

flexiDd_Death.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing death benefit (taxable)

flexiDd_Death_NonTax.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing death benefit (non taxable)

flexiDd_Pension.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing pension (taxable)

flexiDd_PensionCommencementExcess.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing pension commencement excess lump sum (taxable)

flexiDd_PensionCommencementExcess_NonTax.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing pension commencement excess lump sum (non taxable)

flexiDd_Pension_NonTax.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing pension (non taxable)

flexiDd_SeriousIllHealth.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing serious ill health lump sum (taxable)

flexiDd_SeriousIllHealth_NonTax.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing serious ill health lump sum (non taxable)

flexiDd_StandAloneLumpSum.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing stand alone lump sum (taxable)

flexiDd_StandAloneLumpSum_NonTax.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as flexibly accessing stand alone lump sum (non taxable)

foreignTaxAmount.description: [readonly] The amount paid for Foreign Tax Credits in this period

foreignTaxAmountReclaimed.description: [readonly] The amount of Foreign Tax Credits that you actually reclaimed

grossForNi.description: [readonly] The amount of the Gross that is subject to NI

grossForNiNonRti.description: [readonly] The amount of the Gross that is subject to NI but excluded from RTI

grossForTax.description: [readonly] The amount of the Gross that is subject to PAYE

grossNotSubjectToEmployersNi.description:
[readonly] The amount of the Gross that is not subject to Employers NI.
This is the same as GrossForNI where the employees NI Table has an Er contribution

grossNotSubjectToEmployersNiNonRti.description:
[readonly] The amount of the Gross that is not subject to Employers NI.
This is the same as GrossForNI where the employees NI Table has an Er contribution
This is the amount that is excluded from RTI

itemsSubjectToClass1NIC.description: [readonly] Items subject to Class 1 NIC but not taxed under PAYE regulations excluding pension contributions

leavers.description: [readonly] The number of Leavers on ths PayRun

mapsMiles.description: [readonly] The number of miles paid for Mileage Allowance Payments

netPay.description: [readonly] The Net Pay for this Employee

newEmployeesInCurrentSupplementaryPayRun.description: [readonly] The number of new Employees included in the current supplementary PayRun

nilPaid.description: [readonly] The number of employees with NilPaid on the PayRun

nonTaxOrNICPmt.description: [readonly] The value of any payments being made to this Employee that aren't being subjected to PAYE or NI

nonTierablePay.description: [readonly] The value of any pay that shouldn't count towards determining a pension tier.

notionalPay.description: [readonly] The value of adjustments that are of type notional.

p45Gross.description: [readonly] The value P45 Gross which is held on the Employees Opening Balance

p45Tax.description: [readonly] The value of P45 Tax which is held on the Employees Opening Balance

pbik.description: [readonly] Value of any Payrolled Benefits In Kind

pensionableEarnings.description:
[readonly] The amount of the Gross that is subject to Pension Deductions.
If the Pension Scheme uses Qualifying Earnings (upper and lower limits) then this value is before those are applied

pensionablePay.description: [readonly] The amount of the Gross that pension calculations are based on after taking into account Upper and Lower Limits for the WorkerGroup.

postgradLoanRecoveredNonRti.description: [readonly] Postgrad Loan recovered excluded from RTI

realTimeClass1ANiNonRti.description: [readonly] Real Time Class 1A NI excluded from RTI

sapNonRti.description: [readonly] Statutory Adoption Pay excluded from RTI

shppNonRti.description: [readonly] Statutory Shared Parental Pay excluded from RTI

smpNonRti.description: [readonly] Statutory Maternity Pay excluded from RTI

sncpNonRti.description: [readonly] Statutory Neonatal Care Pay excluded from RTI

spbpNonRti.description: [readonly] Statutory Parental Bereavement Pay excluded from RTI

sppNonRti.description: [readonly] Statutory Paternity Pay excluded from RTI

sspNonRti.description: [readonly] Statutory Sick Pay excluded from RTI

starters.description: [readonly] The number of Starters on this PayRun

studentLoanRecoveredNonRti.description: [readonly] Student Loan recovered excluded from RTI

takeHomePay.description: [readonly] The amount this Employee takes home

tcp_Op.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as Trivial Commutation Payment (C - Occupational Pension)

tcp_Pp.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as Trivial Commutation Payment (B - Personal Pension)

tcp_Tcls.description: [readonly] Value of payments marked as Trivial Commutation Payment (A - TCLS)

umbrellaFee.description: [readonly] Value of any Umbrella fee charged for processing the payment

employeeCount.description: [readonly] The number of employees paid with this PaySchedule

employerUniqueId.description: Employer Unique Id for this PaySchedule

firstPeriodEndDate.description: The last day of the first pay period

hasOpenPayRunPeriod.description: [readonly] Will be true if the employer currently has an open PayRun for this PayPeriod

highGrossPay.description: A gross pay amount considered high for this PaySchedule

highNetPay.description: A net pay amount considered high for this PaySchedule

isConfigured.description: [readonly] Whether or not this PaySchedule has been configured and is ready for use

isRequired.description: [readonly] Whether or not this PaySchedule is required for the employer, ie: they have employees to be paid on this PaySchedule

isYearCompleted.description: [readonly] Returns true if all PayRuns for this PaySchedule in the TaxYear have been completed

lastPeriodEndDate.description: [readonly] The end date of the most recent PayRun on this schedule

lastPeriodNumber.description: [readonly] The period number of the most recent PayRun on this schedule

name.description: A descriptive name for this PaySchedule

ordinal.description: [readonly] Indicates whether this is first, second, third (etc) PaySchedule for this PayPeriod.

payRuns.description: [readonly] Details of PayRuns for this PaySchedule

periodEventsConfig.description: Only applicable if Bureau functionality is enabled. Defines the number of days each event occurs before the Payment Date.

periodLengths.description: Only applicable for PayPeriod of Custom. Defines the length of each period

yearEndTasks.description: [readonly] A list of actions that need to be completed for this PaySchedule before this TaxYear can be finalised

endDate.description: [readonly] The end date of the PaySchedulePeriod

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

paySchedulePeriodEvents.description: [readonly] List of all the events in this PaySchedulePeriod

paymentDate.description: The payment date of the PaySchedulePeriod.

period.description: [readonly] The Period number of the PaySchedulePeriod.

startDate.description: [readonly] The start date of the PaySchedulePeriod

unadjustedPaymentDate.description: The payment date of the PaySchedulePeriod when not accounting for weekends, bank hols or ad-hoc alterations to the pay schedule

PaySchedulePeriodEvent.description: An Event within a PaySchedulePeriod. The event could be scheduled x days before PaymentDate.

actualEventDate.description: The actual date and time when the event was completed.

eventDate.description: The expected date and time of the event.

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isOverDue.description: Returns true if its past the event date.

annualDecimals.description: Number of decimal places to calculate Annual Salaries

dailyDecimals.description: Number of decimal places to calculate Daily Rates

dailyDivisor.description: Days used to determine Full time Daily Rate

fullTimeHours.description: Maximum Full Time Hours on this Pay Spine

fullTimeWeeks.description: Maximum Full Time Weeks on this Pay Spine

hourlyDecimals.description: Number of decimal places to calculate Hourly Rates

hourlyDivisor.description: Hours used to determine Hourly Rate

pointEffectiveDate.description: Effective date for applicable spinal points

requiresLondonAllowance.description: Requires LA column to be completed on the spine

salaryFormula.description: Formula used to derive annual salary

hasMinimumBankDetails.description: Denotes whether the payee has the minimum bank details to receive a payment

PaymentsCsvMapping.description:
This model is used to save CSV mappings for importing of payments.
It probably has very little practical use outside of our own UI

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isReferenceOnly.description: If set to true then no PayCode is mapped and this column is just for on-screen refernece only

rateColIndex.description: If the mapping IsMultiplier, then this optionally specifies another column from which to get the value

employeePension.description: The value of the Pension Contribution(PENSION) being made by the Employee

employeePensionContributionReliefAtSourc.description: The value of the Pension Contribution Relief At Source(PENSIONRAS) being made by the Employee

employeePensionContributionSalarySacrifi.description: The value of the Pension Contribution Salary Sacrifice(PENSIONSS) being made by the Employee

pensionSummaryCostBreakdowns.description: List of pension summaries, each representing a breakdown of various pension components

roleId.description: RoleId of that particular line entry

roleWeightage.description: Weightage value of the given employee role

PayrunEmail.description: PayrunEmails are sent automatically when a payrun has been finalised

attachBankPayments.description: If set to True then the Bank Payments CSV file will be attached to the email

attachCostAnalysis.description: If set to True then the Cost Analysis report will be attached to the email

attachCostOfEmployment.description: If set to True then the Cost of Employment report will be attached to the email

attachFullSummaryOfPay.description: If set to True then the Full Summary Of Payment report will be attached to the email

attachGrossToNet.description: If set to True then the GrossToNet report will be attached to the email

attachP30.description: If set to True then the P30 report will be attached to the email

attachP32.description: If set to True then the P32 report will be attached to the email

attachPaymentSummary.description: If set to True then the PaymentSummary report will be attached to the Email

attachPayslips.description: If set to true then Payslips will be attached to the email

attachPensionContributions.description: If set to True then the Pensions Contributions report will be attached to the email

childId.description: This is nothing but the UniqueId of the model.

customBody.description: If TemplateType is null then you can provide the body text here to be used for the email

customBody_IsHtml.description: If the CustomBody is in HTML format, set this to true.

customSubject.description: If TemplateType is null then you can provide a subject line here to be used for the email

isActive.description: If false then this email won't be sent

name.description: Only used for reference, not included in the email

payslips_SingleFile.description: If AttachPayslips is true and this property is also true then the payslips will be attached as a single file rather than as separate PDFs,

payslips_Unemailed.description: If AttachPayslips is true and this property is also true then only payslips that haven't already been sent directly to employees will be attached.

pdfPassword.description: If a value is provided, then all PDFs attached to the email will be encrypted using this password

recipientAddress.description: The address to which the email should be sent

PayrunPaymentsReport.description:
After finalising a PayRun, employees need to actually be paid.
This model is returned by the Reports API and is used to provide details of a payment that needs to be made.

link.description: [readonly]  If available, a link to the payments in an ExternalDataProvider

statusMessage.description: [readonly] A message to elaborate on the Status

Payslip.description:
If you don't want to use our customisable PDFs for Payslips then you can retrieve the raw data used to create a Payslip.
This is the model you will be provided with for each employee.

allowanceNote.description: [readonly] Details of remaining allowance to show on payslip

costCentres.description: The employees Cost Centre Membership(s) only populated when creating journals

departmentMemberships.description: The Employee Department Membership(s) by role only populated when creating journals

depts.description: The employees Department Membership(s) at the time the payrun was finalised

employeeCostCentres.description: The Employee Cost Centre Membership(s) by role only populated when creating journals

employeePensionContributions.description: Dictionary of Pension Contributions(s) Keyed on PensionId containing Employee Pension and AVC Contribution(s) Bfd, AVC indicator and pension rule

employeeRoleAnalysisCategoryCodes.description: The EmployeeRoleAnalysisCategoryCodes only populated when creating journals

employerPensionContributions.description: [readonly] The list of Employer Pension Contribution(s)

from.description: [readonly] The start date of the period this Payslip covers.

isClosed.description: [readonly] Indicates if the Payrun has been finalised

isSupplementary.description: Indicates if this payslip is for a supplementary pay run

lines.description: [readonly] The lines to display on the Payslip

logoUrl.description: [readonly] The Logo to include on the payslip

note.description: [readonly] Any note that should appear on the payslip

paymentDate.description: [readonly] The date the Employee will be paid on

pensionContributionsByRole.description: The Pension Employer contribution by role only populated when creating journals

period.description: [readonly] A description of the period that Payslip relates to.

to.description: [readonly] The end date of the period this Payslip covers.

PayslipCustomisation.description:
Used to represent any customisations you make to the look of Payslip PDFs.
This is covered in detail in the Guides section.

custom.description:
Whether or not you are customising the Payslip.
This should always be true if you are providing any other values.

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

includeBenefits.description: Whether or not to include any payrolled benefits

includeDepartment.description: Whether or not to include the primary department of the employee on the payslip

includeEmployerAddress.description: Whether or not to include the employer address

includeHealthAndSocialCareMessage.description: Whether or not to include health and social car message

includeLogo.description: Whether or not to include the employer logo on the payslip.

removePensionYtd.description: Whether or not to remove the pension YTD column

removeYtd.description: Whether or not to remove the YTD column

useUmbrellaFormat.description: If true then the format the payslip will include details of the Umbrella Payment as well as employer costs

attachmentOrderId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated AttachmentOrder. Only included if the Code is AEO

isNetToGross.description: If the PayLine is a fixed ammount addition without multiplier then this property may be set to true so that the amount of the addition to be considered a take home pay target.

isNotional.description: Determines whether the transaction involves non-monetary benefits for payroll processing, potentially subject to tax and National Insurance

loanId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated Loan, if any.

multiplier.description: [readonly] If the Code is a multiplier code then this is the multiplier. Value has already been calculated so this is just for reference

pensionId.description: [readonly] The Id of the associated Pension. Only included if the Code is PENSION, PENSIONSS or PENSIONRAS

roleId.description:
The employee role which is associated with this payslip line.
If set to null, the payslip line is not related with a role.

targetNetToGrossValue.description: The orginal net fixed addition amount that is considered to be a take home pay target.

PayslipReportResponse.description:
Used to encapsulate a response for any of the reports.
See the Introduction Guide for Reports for more details

content.description: [readonly] This could contain a link to a PDF file, HTML content or other content, depending on the Type value.

type.description: [readonly] The content-type, this would usually be the same as the accept header you provided when you requested the report

additionalVoluntaryContribution.description: Any additional voluntary amount the employer contributes towards the pension. Could be a percentage or a fixed amount depending on AvcIsPercentage.

associatedEmployeeRoles.description: The list of employee roles associated with the pension membership

autoEnrolled.description: [readonly] Is True if the employee joined this Pension due to an AutoEnrolment action

avcIsPercentage.description: Determines whether the Value of the Additional Voluntary Contribution is a fixed amount or a percentage,

employeeContribution.description:
The amount the employee contributes towards the pension. Could be a percentage or a fixed amount depending on EmployeeContributionIsPercentage.
This is read-only if OverrideContributions is false

employeeContributionIsPercentage.description: Determines whether the Value of the EmployeeContribution is a fixed amount or a percentage,

employerContribution.description:
The amount the employer contributes towards the pension. Could be a percentage or a fixed amount depending on EmployerContributionIsPercentage.
This is read-only if OverrideContributions is false

employerContributionIncludesNiSaving.description: Employer Contribution includes the Employers NI saving

employerContributionIsPercentage.description: Determines whether the Value of the EmployerContribution is a fixed amount or a percentage,

employerContributionNiSavingPercentage.description: Increase Employer Contribution by this percentage of the Employers NI saving

employerContributionTopUpPercentage.description: Increase Employer Contribution by this percentage of the Employee Contribution

endDate.description: The date the employee left the scheme

endPayRunEntryId.description: [readonly] Holds the Id for the payroll that the employee left the scheme

fiftyFiftySchemeMember.description: if an employee as a member of the 50/50 LGPS scheme, they can enable contribution to 50% of the normal contributions.

forceEnrolment.description: If the PensionScheme is connected to an ExternalDataProvider that supports enrolment then setting this to true will force this employee to be enrolled with the next submission.

forcedTier.description:
If the WorkerGroup ContributionLevelType is a Tiered Scheme then you can specify the name of the tier to force the employee on to.
If none is specified then the Tier is determined by the earnings in the period

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

isAeQualifyingScheme.description: [readonly] Whether or not the associated PensionScheme is a Qualifying Scheme for AutoEnrolment

isTeachersPension.description: [readonly] Whether or not the associated PensionScheme is a Teachers' Pension (determined by its CsvFormat)

overrideContributions.description: If this is set to true then the Contributions levels set for the WorkerGroup will be replaced with the values provided here

reasonForLeaving.description: If a value is provided for EndDate then this is the reason for leaving the employee left the scheme

startPayRunEntryId.description: [readonly] Holds the Id for the payroll that the employee joined the scheme

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

PensionContributionsSubmission.description: This model is used to track submission of Pension Contributions to an external data provider.

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

assumedPensionablePayYTD.description: Assumed pensionable pay year to date

isAvcPercentage.description: Indicates whether the AvcContribution is expressed as a percentage.

isEmployeeContributionPercentage.description: Indicates whether the EmployeeContribution is expressed as a percentage.

isEmployerContributionPercentage.description: Indicates whether the EmployerContribution is expressed as a percentage.

isPensionJoinerActionReported.description: Indicates whether the pension joiner  action is reported.

isPensionLeaverActionReported.description: Indicates whether the pension leaver  action is reported.

joinDate.description: The Join Date of the employee

mcrRoleID1.description: MCR Role ID1 for teachers pension

mcrRoleID2.description: MCR Role ID2 for teachers pension

reasonForLeaving.description: Reason the employee left the scheme

workerGroup.description: The WorkerGroup of the employee pension scheme

PensionMembershipsReportReportResponse.description:
Used to encapsulate a response for any of the reports.
See the Introduction Guide for Reports for more details

content.description: [readonly] This could contain a link to a PDF file, HTML content or other content, depending on the Type value.

type.description: [readonly] The content-type, this would usually be the same as the accept header you provided when you requested the report

assumedPensionablePay.description: Opening balances for assumed pensionable pay

employeePensionContribution.description: Opening balances for employee pension contribution

employeePensionContributionAvc.description: Opening balances for employee pension contribution avc

employerPensionContribution.description: Opening balances for employer pension contribution

excludeNilPaidFromContributions.description:
If we're sending contributions to an external provider then we'll include all employees that were on the payrun.
If you want to exclude employees that don't have any contributions to report then set this to true.

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

miscBoolean1.description:
This field has different uses dependent on the ExternalDataProvider, if any.
For Nest, it indicates whether or not contributions are reported as "Tax Weekly/Monthly" rather than just "Weekly/Monthly"

miscBoolean2.description:
This field has different uses dependent on the ExternalDataProvider, if any.
For Nest, it indicates whether or not to approve payments after submitting contributions

miscString1.description:
This field has different uses dependent on the ExternalDataProvider, if any.
For Nest, it dictates the PaymentSource.

miscString2.description: This field has different uses dependent on the ExternalDataProvider, if any.

optOutWindow.description: The number of days or months that an employee has to Opt out after being enrolled

optOutWindowIsMonths.description: Determines whether the value given for OptOutWindow is in Months (true) or days (false)

payPeriodDateAdjustment.description:
If you need to adjust the reported dates of the contributions then you can do so by setting a non-zero value here.
A negative value of will move the date back in time.

PensionRefund.description: Used to represent a Pension Refund

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

customPayCodes.description:
If UseCustomPayCodes is set to true then this contains a list of PayCodes.Code
that we'll treat as being pensionable.

deficitValue.description: The Defict value that will be deducted in a PaySchedule

deficitValueOrdinal.description: The Ordinal used to identify the PaySchedule which the Deficit will be against

disableAeLetters.description: Set to true if the provider deals with AutoEnrolment Letters and therefore the system should not generate them

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

includeDeficitValue.description: Set to true if this a Deficit Payment needs to be assigned to a PaySchedule

nominalCodeMappings.description: The PensionSchemeNominalCodeMappings used to map the PayCodes to a PensionScheme for the journal configuration

qualifyingScheme.description: Set to true if this is a Qualifying Scheme for Auto Enrolment

tierablePayCustomPayCodes.description:
If UseCustomPayCodesForTierablePay is set to true then this contains a list of PayCodes.Code
that we'll treat as being tierable.

useCustomPayCodes.description:
If set to true then rather than using the setting on the PayCode to determine if the pay is pensionable
we'll instead treat it as pensionable if the Code is included in CustomPayCodes

useCustomPayCodesForTierablePay.description:
If set to true then rather than using the setting on the PayCode to determine if the pay is tierable
we'll instead treat it as tierable if the Code is included in TierablePayCustomPayCodes

workerGroups.description: A list of WorkerGroups for this Pension. There must always be at least one WorkerGroup

PensionSummary.description:
If a PayRunEntry contains pension contributions then it'll also include a PensionSummary model
giving further information about the Pension Scheme and the contributions made

additionalVoluntaryContribution.description:
[readonly] Any Additional Voluntary Contribution the Employee has chosen to make
Otherwise it is set as 1.

assumedPensionablePay.description:
[readonly] Assumed Pensionable Pay. If the employee is receiving any Statutory Leave that has an AssumedPensionablePay value set
then it'll be shown here.

assumedPensionablePayBfd.description:
[readonly] The brought forward Year to Date value of Assumed Pensionable Pay. If the employee is receiving any Statutory Leave that has an AssumedPensionablePay value set
then it'll be shown here.

autoEnrolled.description:
[readonly] Any Additional Voluntary Contribution the Employee has chosen to make
Otherwise it is set as 1.

avcIsPercentage.description: [readonly] Determines whether the Value of the Additional Voluntary Contribution is a fixed amount or a percentage,

employeePensionContribution.description: [readonly] The value of the Pension Contribution being made by this Employee, excluding any Additional Voluntary Contributions

employeePensionContributionAvc.description: [readonly] The value of the Pension Contribution being made by this Employee as an Additional Voluntary Contribution

employeePensionContributionAvcBfd.description: [readonly] The brought forward Year to Date value of the Pension Contribution being made by this Employee as an Additional Voluntary Contribution

employeePensionContributionBfd.description: [readonly] The brought forward Year to Date value of the Pension Contribution being made by this Employee, excluding any Additional Voluntary Contributions

employeePensionContributionMultiplier.description:
[readonly] If the PensionScheme is set to SubtractBasicRateTax then this value  is used to reduce the contribution amount.
Otherwise it is set as 1.

employerPensionContribution.description: [readonly] The value of the Pension Contribution being made by the Employer for this Employee

employerPensionContributionBfd.description: [readonly] The brought forward Year to Date value of the Pension Contribution being made by the Employer for this Employee

forcedTier.description:
[readonly] If the WorkerGroup ContributionLevelType is a Tiered Scheme then the name of the tier to force the employee on to may be specified.
If none is specified then the Tier is determined by the earnings in the period

isForEndedPension.description: [readonly] Determines whether the pension summary is related to a pension that has ended or not

isHalfContributionMember.description: if an employee as a member of the 50/50 LGPS scheme, they can enable contribution to 50% of the normal contributions.

leaveDaysUnderHalfPay.description: The numer of leave days where less than half the normal pay is received

name.description: [readonly] The name of the PensionScheme to which contributions have been made.

nominalCodeMappings.description: List of Nominal Code Mappings of the Pension

nonTierablePay.description: [readonly] The value of any pay that shouldn't count towards determining a pension tier.

notionalPay.description:
Pay value based on regular pay lines and the pension's associated roles
Set before any adjustments for things like stat payments

pensionableEarnings.description:
[readonly] The amount of the Gross that is subject to Pension Deductions.
If the Pension Scheme uses Qualifying Earnings (upper and lower limits) then this value is before those are applied
Applied only if an employee has more than one pension assigned to them

pensionableEarningsBfd.description:
[readonly] The brought forward Year to Date amount of the Gross that is subject to Pension Deductions.
Applied only if an employee has more than one pension assigned to them

pensionablePay.description:
[readonly] The amount of the Gross that pension calculations are based on after taking into account Upper and Lower Limits for the WorkerGroup.
Applied only if an employee has more than one pension assigned to them

pensionablePayBfd.description:
[readonly] The brought forward Year to Date amount of the Gross that pension calculations are based on after taking into account Upper and Lower Limits for the WorkerGroup.
Applied only if an employee has more than one pension assigned to them

pensionablePayCodes.description: [readonly] If the pension scheme is set to override the Pensionale PayCodes, then this is what they've been set to.

periodFrom.description:
Nullable DateTime property "PeriodFrom" to store the start date of the PayPeriod the current PensionSummary is attributable to.
Applicable only for when the pension summary is created on the base of when earned additions' and deductions' pension calculations for back paid periods

periodTo.description:
Nullable DateTime property "PeriodTo" to store the start date of the PayPeriod the current PensionSummary is attributable to.
Applicable only for when the pension summary is created on the base of when earned additions' and deductions' pension calculations for back paid periods

subtractedBasicRateTax.description: [readonly] The SubtractedBasicRateTax applied from the PensionScheme SubtractBasicRateTax.

tierablePayPayCodes.description: [readonly] If the pension scheme is set to override the TierablePay PayCodes, then this is what they've been set to.

treatAsWhenPaidForBackPay.description: [readonly] Determines whether the pension summary should be treated as when paid when the earnings are based only on back pay

whenEarnedEmployeePensionContributionAvc.description: [readonly] The when earned amount correction to Pension Contribution being made by this Employee as an Additional Voluntary Contribution
[readonly] The brought forward Year to Date when earned amount correction to Pension Contribution being made by this Employee as an Additional Voluntary Contribution

whenEarnedEmployeePensionContributionCor.description: [readonly] The when earned amount correction to the Pension Contribution being made by this Employee, excluding any Additional Voluntary Contributions
[readonly] The brought forward Year to Date when earned amount correction to the Pension Contribution being made by this Employee, excluding any Additional Voluntary Contributions

whenEarnedEmployerPensionContributionCor.description: [readonly] The when earned amount correction to the Pension Contribution being made by the Employer for this Employee
[readonly] The brought forward Year to Date when earned amount correction to the Pension Contribution being made by the Employer for this Employee

whenEarnedPensionSummaries.description: The list of pension summaries created on the base of when earned additions' and deductions' pension calculations for back paid periods

whenEarnedPensionableEarningsCorrection.description: [readonly] The when earned amount correction to the Gross that is subject to Pension Deductions.

whenEarnedPensionableEarningsCorrectionB.description: [readonly] The brought forward Year to Date when earned amount correction to the Gross that is subject to Pension Deductions.

whenEarnedPensionablePayCorrection.description: [readonly] The when earned amount correction to the Gross that pension calculations are based on after taking into account Upper and Lower Limits for the WorkerGroup.

whenEarnedPensionablePayCorrectionBfd.description: [readonly] The brought forward Year to Date when earned amount correction to the Gross that pension calculations are based on after taking into account Upper and Lower Limits for the WorkerGroup.

amount.description: The amount associated with this pension entry.

employeePensionContribution.description: The contribution amount made by the employee towards the pension.

employerPensionContribution.description: The contribution amount made by the employer towards the pension.

payCodes.description: The list of pay codes associated with this pension rule.

pensionableEarnings.description: The total pensionable earnings relevant to this pension rule.

bereaved.description: Indicator that Occupational Pension is being paid because they are a recently bereaved Spouse/Civil Partner

inReceiptOfPension.description: If set to true then the FPS will have the OccPenInd flag set to 'yes'

effectiveFrom.description: The date when these period values are effective

midYearChanges.description: Any optional changes to the period values that are effective from a specified date in the middle of a tax year

periodValues.description: Period values for different pay periods

emailPayslip.description: If set to true then the employees Payslip will be sent by email when a PayRun is finalised.

emailStatement.description: Only applicable to CIS Subcontractors. If set to true then we will automatically email a CIS Statement when a CIS300 is accepted.

pdfPassword.description: Set the password to be used on PDFs. If blank then we'll create a password based on the PdfPasswordType property.

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object
[readonly] The unique id of the object

completedDate.description: UpdatedDate is assigned to this to get around the base implementation of UpdatedDate having [JsonIgnore] tag

documentCount.description: [readonly] The number of attachments associated with this model

documents.description: [readonly] The attachments associated with this model

id.description: [readonly] The unique id of the object

payRunEntryId.description: The Id of the payrunEntry this ProcessingNote relates to, if any

updatedByName.description: Name of the user who was last to update the record

cisDeductionsSuffered.description: Value of CIS deductions suffered year to date

nicCompensationOnSAP.description: Value of NIC compensation on Statutory Adoption Pay year to date

nicCompensationOnSMP.description: Value of NIC compensation on SMP year to date

nicCompensationOnSNCP.description: Value of NIC compensation on Statutory Neonatal Care Pay year to date

nicCompensationOnSPBP.description: Value of NIC compensation on Statutory Parental Bereavement Pay year to date

nicCompensationOnSPP.description: Value of NIC compensation on Statutory Paternity Pay year to date

nicCompensationOnShPP.description: Value of NIC compensation on Shared Parental Pay year to date

sapRecovered.description: Value of Statutory Adoption Pay recovered year to date

shPPRecovered.description: Value of Shared Parental Pay recovered year to date

smpRecovered.description: Value of Statutory Maternity Pay recovered year to date

sncpRecovered.description: Value of Statutory Neonatal Care Pay recovered year to date

spbpRecovered.description: Value of Statutory Parental Bereavement Pay recovered year to date

sppRecovered.description: Value of Statutory Paternity Pay recovered year to date

total.description: The total value of the reclaimed amounts

reports.description: The Reports included in this ReportPack

allowLinkedEps.description: If set to true this will allow you to submit a combined Employer Payment Summary

autoSubmitEps.description: If set to true, we'll automatically send your EPS to HMRC whenever you finalise a PayRun.

autoSubmitFps.description: If set to true, we'll automatically send your FPS to HMRC whenever you finalise a PayRun

[readonly] Whether or not the Bacs Cross Ref field is included on your FPS submissions
This is automatically set to true if you use a bank payments CSV format that supports it
or set to false if not

senderId.description: The SenderId used to submit RTI documents to HMRC

useTestGateway.description: If set to true then your RTI documents will be sent to HMRCs test services instead of the live service

Tenant.description:
The Tenant model represents the brand that provides the account.
This is used by our White Label partners to manage and brand their user accounts.
Unless you are an admin for a White Label account you'll have no interest in this model.

disableEvc.description: Indicates whether FPS should be submitted to EVC for employees/employers under this tenant.

enableAdminsToManageReportWriter.description: Indicates whether all Admin users of a tenant can manage report templates.

enableReportWriterFeature.description: Indicates if the Report Writer feature is enabled for the tenant.

Represents a User Account.
As well as basic details about the user it also includes details of Employers that the user account can access.

[readonly] If false then the user cannot create new employers.
This can be turned on or off by the tenant admin.

DisabledReason will give a reason why the account is disabled

address it'll be changed to after verification will be shown here.

This model provides information about what the User is able to access.
This would usually just be a list of Employers. But if the user is an administrator for a White Label instance then this will be shown in the list of Tenants.

This property will automatically be set to true for the following Contribution Level Types: TpFasterAccrual, TpAdditionalPensionContributions, TpActuariallyAdjustedBenefits, TpFamilyBenefits, tpPastAddedYears, tpHigherSalaries, tpPreston, tpElectedFurtherEmployment, LgpsAdditionalPensionContributions, LgpsSharedAdditionalPensionContributions, LgpsAdditionalRegularContributions, LgpsAddedYearsContributions, LgpsSharedAdditionalPensionLumpSump, LgpsPartTimeBuyBack, PrudentialAVC.

get.description:
Calculates the assumed pensionable pay for a specific employee role during a leave period.
For existing leaves (when id is provided), uses the leave's existing configuration.
For new leaves (when id is empty), calculates based on the provided leave type, pay type, and period parameters.
Only supports roles associated with LGPS schemes that allow automatic APP calculation.

parameters.name: employerId

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the employee
parameters.name: employeeId

parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the employee role for which to calculate assumed pensionable pay
parameters.name: roleId

parameters.description: The pay period (required for new leaves, ignored for existing leaves)
parameters.name: payPeriod

parameters.description: The type of leave (required for new leaves, ignored for existing leaves)
parameters.name: leaveType

parameters.description: The leave payment type (required for new leaves, ignored for existing leaves)
parameters.name: leavePayType

parameters.description: The leave start date (required for new leaves, ignored for existing leaves)
parameters.name: from

put.description:
Updates the P45 Values on the Opening Balances for an Employee.
This would ideally be done when the Opening Balances are updated but if payruns have already been started for the employee then Opening Balances can't be updated - hence this API call just to update the P45 values.
There must be a currently open PayRun for the employee.
Only the PreviousEmployerGross and PreviousEmployerTax properties of the submitted OpeningBalances model are updated.

parameters.description: The Id of the Employee for which you want to update P45 figures

Only the TerminationPayments property of the submitted OpeningBalances model is updated.

get.description:
For ExternalDataProviders with an AuthScheme of OAuth1 or OAuth2.
Returns a Url to redirect a user to in order to start the authorization process with the given ExternalDataProvider.
Our API handles the response from the OAuth provider and processes it to obtain a token.
The user is then sent to the URL specified.

parameters.name: employerId

parameters.description: The Id of the ExternalDataProvider you want to authorize with.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The URL to send the user to once the process is complete.
parameters.name: returnUrl

parameters.name: id

parameters.description: The password for the ExternalDataProvider.

post.description:
Takes a list PayOptionsImport and updates the currently open payrun to use the amounts given.
You must have an open payrun for the PayPeriod specified and all payroll codes submitted must match an employee on the payrun.
Any lines imported for a PayRunEntry will replace existing lines for that PayRunEntry.

parameters.description: If set to true then only lines will be imported. Changes to regular pay will be ignored. Defaults to false.
parameters.name: LinesOnly

parameters.description: If set to true then the lines submitted will be appended to existing lines rather than replace them. Defaults to false.
parameters.name: Append

parameters.description: If set to true then any submitted lines that match the pay code of one (and only one) existing line will be used to update the value of that lines. Any unmatched lines will be appended to existing lines. Defaults to false.
parameters.name: UpdateExisting

post.description:
Registers the request to start the next PayRun and returns a 202 if successful
This endpoint is currently being beta tested and subject to un-announced breaking changes.

202.description:
Success - the request to start a PayRun has been accepted.

post.description:
Takes a list PayOptionsImport and updates the currently open payrun to use the amounts given.
You must have an open payrun for the PayPeriod specified and all payroll codes submitted must match an employee on the payrun.
Any lines imported for a PayRunEntry will replace existing lines for that PayRunEntry.

parameters.description: If set to true then only lines will be imported. Changes to regular pay will be ignored. Defaults to false.
parameters.name: linesOnly

parameters.description: If set to true then the lines submitted will be appended to existing lines rather than replace them. Defaults to false.
parameters.name: append

parameters.description: If set to true then any submitted lines that match the pay code of one (and only one) existing line will be used to update the value of that lines. Any unmatched lines will be appended to existing lines.
parameters.name: updateExisting

parameters.description: If set to true, the import will only be validated for data correctness. If false, the import is validated and then imported.
parameters.name: validateOnly

post.description:
If the employer is connected to an ExternalDataProvider that provides Time and Attendance data then this API
call will update the currently open payrun for the PayPeriod specified with data from the specified provider.
Returns a list of PayOptionsImport to show what's been imported.

Takes a list UmbrellaPayment and updates the currently open payrun to use the amounts given.
You must have an open payrun for the PayPeriod specified and all payroll codes submitted must match an employee on the payrun.
You should have also set the UmbrellaSettings for the Employer

parameters.description: The Id of the Employer to which the Employee belongs.
parameters.name: employerId

parameters.description: The Id of the Employee you want to get pay run entry for.
parameters.name: employeeId

requestBody.description:
Specify the PayRun state you want to change to and the reason for the change.
Only Open and Finalised states are available at the moment.
Other states are used with Bureau functionality which isn't currently generally available.

200.description: Success - the PayRun has been updated.

post.description:
Payslips are usually emailed automatically when a PayRun is finalised. But if you suppressed them or need to re-send them then you can do so with this API call.
Providing a pay run entry id will send a single email, otherwise all emails will be sent.

parameters.name: taxYear

parameters.description: The ordinal number of the pay run.
parameters.name: ordinal

parameters.description: The UniqueId of the pay run entry (optional).
parameters.name: id

post.description:
This endpoint is now deprecated and will be removed in Jan 2022.
You should instead use the Update method and set the State to Finalised.
            
Returns True if the resulting FPS has been automatically submitted due to the Employers RTISubmissionSettings, otherwise returns false.
You can suppress the emailing of payslips to employees by adding a key named dontEmailEmployeePayslips with a value of true to the body. See the related guides for more information.

parameters.description: Set to true to display Apprenticeship Levy amount for all schedules; otherwise, only for the selected schedule in the journal
parameters.name: allSchedules

get.description:
Returns a List of Items representing any Leave within the PayRun date range for Employees that are included in the given PayRun.
Note that if Leave was added after the PayRun was closed, but the Leave is within the PayRun period then it'll be returned here even though it wouldn't have been processed with the PayRun.
get.summary: List Leave related to a PayRun

post.description:
Sets the PaymentDate for a PayRun.

PayRunEntries is updated, the PayRun will retain it's current PaymentDate
